# Payviox Documentation

> Payviox is a payment infrastructure for online businesses. Accept payments via cards, local payment methods, and crypto. Integrate using the JavaScript SDK (client-side) or the REST API (server-side). Receive payment confirmations via webhooks. All amounts are in cents (e.g. 5000 = $50.00).

## Product Overview

- Website: https://payviox.com
- Dashboard: https://dash.payviox.com
- API base URL: https://api.payviox.com
- Payment page: https://secure.payviox.com/{session_id}
- SDK CDN: https://sdk.payviox.com/payviox.js
- Docs: https://docs.payviox.com
- Support: support@payviox.com

### API Key Types

| Key type | Prefix | Use from | IP handling |
|----------|--------|----------|-------------|
| Public API Key | `pk_` | Browser / SDK | Auto-captured from request |
| Secret API Key | `sk_` | Backend server | Optional `ip` param for fraud prevention |

Both key types work with the same endpoints. Secret keys unlock the `ip` parameter on session creation. Test keys use prefix `pk_test_` / `sk_test_`.

Authentication: `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY`

---

## Integration Decision Guide

### RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: JavaScript SDK with redirect mode

Use when: any web app, SPA, or website where you want payments working in minutes.

Why:
- Simplest integration (5-10 lines of code)
- No backend required
- Full PCI compliance (payment data never touches your server)
- Customer is redirected to Payviox-hosted secure payment page

### USE SDK iframe mode ONLY IF:

- The user explicitly wants an embedded payment form (no page redirect)
- AND their domain can be whitelisted in the Payviox dashboard

### USE REST API ONLY IF:

- Backend-only application (no browser), OR
- Mobile app backend, OR
- Non-JavaScript tech stack (Python, PHP, Ruby, Go, etc.), OR
- Need server-side control over session creation (e.g. IP fraud prevention), OR
- Need direct provider redirect (skip Payviox payment page entirely)

### USE REST API with paymentMethodId IF:

- You want to bypass the Payviox payment page and send the customer directly to a specific payment provider (Zen, Nicepay, PayPal, Payssion, Pallapay, Crypto.com)
- The API returns a `redirect_url` instead of just a `session_id`

### Decision Summary

| Scenario | Recommended approach |
|----------|---------------------|
| Simple web checkout | SDK redirect mode |
| Embedded payment form | SDK iframe mode |
| Backend creates sessions | REST API + redirect to `https://secure.payviox.com/{session_id}` |
| Skip Payviox payment page | REST API with `paymentMethodId` for a redirect-based provider |
| Mobile app | REST API from your backend |
| Non-JS stack | REST API |
| Distributing funds to users | Payout API |

---

## JavaScript SDK Reference

### Installation

CDN (recommended):
```html
<script src="https://sdk.payviox.com/payviox.js"></script>
```

NPM:
```bash
npm install @payviox/sdk
```

```javascript
import Payviox from '@payviox/sdk';
```

### Initialization

```javascript
const payviox = new Payviox('pk_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx');
```

The constructor takes your public API key (string). The SDK auto-configures the API endpoint (https://api.payviox.com) and payment page URL (https://secure.payviox.com).

### createSession(sessionParameters, options?) -> Promise<void | string>

Creates a payment session. Returns `void` if redirect is enabled (page redirects immediately), or a `string` session ID otherwise.

#### SessionParameters (all required):

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `amount` | number | Amount in cents. Min 100 ($1.00). Must equal sum of items. |
| `currency` | string | ISO currency code: `USD`, `EUR`, `GBP`, etc. |
| `customer` | string | Customer identifier (email or ID) |
| `description` | string | Human-readable payment description |
| `paymentMethodId` | string | Payment method ID. Use `pm_card` for cards, or get IDs from `getPaymentsMethods()`. |
| `order_id` | string | Unique order identifier for your business |
| `items` | array | Array of `{ name: string, quantity: number, price: number }`. Price is per unit in cents. |

#### PayvioxParameters (optional):

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `redirect` | boolean | false | Auto-redirect to payment page. Cannot be used with `iframeMode`. |
| `iframeMode` | boolean | false | Embed payment in iframe. Requires `iframeConfig`. |
| `iframeConfig` | object | - | Iframe settings (see below). Required when `iframeMode` is true. |

#### IframeConfig:

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `iframeTarget` | HTMLElement | required | DOM element to insert iframe into |
| `width` | string | "100%" | CSS width |
| `height` | string | "400px" | CSS height |
| `withoutBorder` | boolean | false | Remove iframe border |
| `transparentBackground` | boolean | false | Transparent background |
| `style` | string | "" | Additional CSS styles |

#### Complete Example — Redirect Mode:

```javascript
const payviox = new Payviox('pk_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx');

await payviox.createSession({
  amount: 5000,
  currency: 'USD',
  customer: 'customer_123',
  description: 'Premium Plan',
  paymentMethodId: 'pm_card',
  order_id: 'order_' + Date.now(),
  items: [{ name: 'Premium Plan', quantity: 1, price: 5000 }]
}, {
  redirect: true
});
```

#### Complete Example — Iframe Mode:

```javascript
const payviox = new Payviox('pk_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx');

await payviox.createSession({
  amount: 5000,
  currency: 'USD',
  customer: 'customer_123',
  description: 'Premium Plan',
  paymentMethodId: 'pm_card',
  order_id: 'order_' + Date.now(),
  items: [{ name: 'Premium Plan', quantity: 1, price: 5000 }]
}, {
  iframeMode: true,
  iframeConfig: {
    iframeTarget: document.getElementById('payment-container'),
    width: '100%',
    height: '600px',
    withoutBorder: true
  }
});
```

#### Direct Provider Redirect:

When `paymentMethodId` is for a redirect-based provider (zen_card, paypal, nicepay_va, etc.), the SDK redirects the customer directly to the provider's page, skipping Payviox's payment page.

```javascript
await payviox.createSession({
  amount: 5000,
  currency: 'USD',
  customer: 'customer_123',
  description: 'Premium Plan',
  paymentMethodId: 'zen_card',
  order_id: 'order_' + Date.now(),
  items: [{ name: 'Premium Plan', quantity: 1, price: 5000 }]
}, { redirect: true });
```

### openSession(sessionId) -> void

Manually redirects the user to `https://secure.payviox.com/{sessionId}`. Use when you need to do something between session creation and redirect.

```javascript
const sessionId = await payviox.createSession(params);
// ... do something ...
payviox.openSession(sessionId);
```

### openIframe(sessionId, iframeConfig) -> void

Manually opens an iframe for an existing session. Same `iframeConfig` as above.

### getPaymentsMethods(filters?) -> Promise<PaymentMethod[]>

Returns available payment methods, optionally filtered.

```javascript
const methods = await payviox.getPaymentsMethods({
  currencies: 'USD',
  countries: 'US'
});
```

Filters: `currencies` (string | string[]), `countries` (string | string[]).

Response type:
```typescript
interface PaymentMethod {
  id: string;        // Use as paymentMethodId
  name: string;      // Display name
  type: string;      // "card", "bank_transfer", "wallet", etc.
  currencies: string[];
  countries: string[];
}
```

---

## REST API Reference

Base URL: `https://api.payviox.com`
Auth: `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY`

### POST /session — Create Payment Session

Creates a payment session. Accepts both public and secret API keys.

Request body:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `amount` | integer | yes | Amount in cents. Min 100. Must equal sum of items. |
| `currency` | string | yes | ISO currency code |
| `customer` | string | yes | Customer identifier |
| `order_id` | string | yes | Unique order ID |
| `description` | string | no | Payment description |
| `items` | array | yes | `[{ name, quantity, price }]` |
| `metadata` | object | no | Custom key-value pairs |
| `ip` | string | no | Client IP (secret key only). If provided, IP is validated at payment time. |
| `paymentMethodId` | string | no | If set to a redirect-based provider, response includes `redirect_url`. |

Response (201):
```json
{ "session_id": "a1b2c3d4e5f6..." }
```

With paymentMethodId for redirect provider:
```json
{ "session_id": "a1b2c3d4e5f6...", "redirect_url": "https://provider.com/pay/..." }
```

If payment initiation fails:
```json
{ "session_id": "a1b2c3d4e5f6...", "payment_error": { "success": false, "message": "...", "error_type": "..." } }
```

Error responses: 400 (validation), 401 (unauthorized), 429 (rate limited with `retry_after` in seconds).

After creating a session, redirect the customer to: `https://secure.payviox.com/{session_id}`

### GET /payments/methods — Get Payment Methods

Query params: `countries` (comma-separated), `currencies` (comma-separated), `is_company` (boolean).

Response (200): Object where keys are payment method IDs:
```json
{
  "stripe_credit": {
    "name": "Credit Card (Stripe)",
    "image": "https://cdn.payviox.com/images/stripe.png",
    "countries": null,
    "currencies": null,
    "fees": { "static_fees": 0.30, "percent_fees": 0.029 }
  }
}
```

`null` for countries/currencies means globally available. Fee formula: `total_fee = static_fees + (amount_in_dollars * percent_fees)`.

### GET /balance — Get Business Balance

Requires secret API key.

Response (200):
```json
{ "balance": 1500000, "payout_balance": 500000 }
```

Both values in cents.

---

## Payout API Reference

The Payout API lets you allocate funds to users who can then withdraw via gift cards, crypto, PayPal, etc.

### POST /payout/create — Create or Fund Payout User

Auth: `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY` (secret key)

Request body:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `email` | string | yes | Payout user email (unique per business) |
| `amount` | integer | yes | Amount in cents to allocate (debited from your payout_balance) |

Response (201):
```json
{ "hash": "a1b2c3d4e5f6..." }
```

The hash is used by the payout user to access their funds. If the email already exists, the amount is added to their existing balance.

Errors: 400 (validation / insufficient balance), 401 (unauthorized), 403 (payouts disabled).

### Payout User Authentication

Payout users authenticate with their hash via: `X-Payout-Hash: HASH` header (recommended), or `payout_hash` in body/query.

---

## Payment Webhooks

Payviox sends HTTP POST webhooks to your configured URL when payment events occur. Configure in Dashboard > Settings > Webhooks.

### Event Types

| Event | Meaning | Action |
|-------|---------|--------|
| `succeeded` | Payment completed | Fulfill the order |
| `pending_review` | Flagged for fraud review | Do NOT fulfill yet — wait for succeeded or declined |
| `declined` | Declined by fraud prevention | Customer auto-refunded. Do not fulfill. |
| `refunded` | Refund processed | Handle refund logic |
| `expired` | Session expired without payment | Optional cleanup |

### Payload Structure

```json
{
  "amount": 10000,
  "currency": "USD",
  "fees": 350,
  "metadata": { "integration_session_id": "sess_abc123" },
  "type": "succeeded",
  "provider": "stripe",
  "order_id": "order_123456",
  "items": [{ "name": "Premium", "quantity": 1, "price": 10000 }],
  "payment_method": "card",
  "customer": {
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@example.com",
    "phone": "+1234567890",
    "address": { "line1": "123 Main St", "city": "Paris", "postal_code": "75001", "country": "FR" }
  }
}
```

The `customer` object is optional and only present when data was collected by the provider. The `fees` field represents additional fees the customer pays on top of the amount.

### Signature Verification

Header: `Signature` — HMAC-SHA256 of the raw request body using your webhook token.

```javascript
const crypto = require('crypto');

function verifySignature(rawBody, signature, webhookToken) {
  const computed = crypto.createHmac('sha256', webhookToken).update(rawBody).digest('hex');
  return crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(computed), Buffer.from(signature));
}
```

```python
import hmac, hashlib

def verify_signature(raw_body, signature, webhook_token):
    computed = hmac.new(webhook_token.encode(), raw_body.encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
    return hmac.compare_digest(computed, signature)
```

```php
function verifySignature($rawBody, $signature, $webhookToken) {
    $computed = hash_hmac('sha256', $rawBody, $webhookToken);
    return hash_equals($computed, $signature);
}
```

### Retry Policy

4 attempts: immediate, then 30s, 5min, 30min. Success = HTTP 200 within 30 seconds. After 4 failures, marked as failed (retryable from dashboard).

### Best Practices

- Always verify signature before processing
- Return 200 OK immediately, process asynchronously
- Implement idempotency using `order_id` + `type`
- Never fulfill orders with `pending_review` status — wait for final event

---

## Payout Webhooks

Separate URL and token from payment webhooks. Configure in Dashboard > Payout > Webhooks.

### Event Types

| Event | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `payout.created` | Payout order created |
| `payout.processing` | Payout being sent |
| `payout.succeeded` | Payout delivered |
| `payout.failed` | Payout failed |
| `payout.rejected` | Payout rejected (may include `reason` field) |

### Payload Structure

Same pattern as payment webhooks: amounts on root, provider-specific data in a nested object keyed by provider name (`paypal` or `crypto`).

```json
{
  "amount": 1000,
  "currency": "USD",
  "fees": 20,
  "net_amount": 980,
  "metadata": { "payout_user_id": "usr_..." },
  "type": "payout.succeeded",
  "provider": "paypal",
  "order_id": "ord_...",
  "recipient": { "email": "user@example.com" },
  "paypal": { "order_id": "PP-...", "email": "recipient@example.com" }
}
```

Signature verification is identical to payment webhooks but uses the payout webhook token. Same retry policy (4 attempts).

---

## Common Integration Patterns

### E-commerce Checkout (SDK Redirect)

1. Include SDK via CDN
2. On "Pay" button click, call `createSession()` with `redirect: true`
3. Customer pays on Payviox page
4. Webhook `succeeded` fires → fulfill order
5. Customer redirected back to your site

### SaaS Subscription (REST API Server-Side)

1. Backend calls `POST /session` with secret key + customer IP
2. Return `session_id` to frontend
3. Frontend redirects to `https://secure.payviox.com/{session_id}`
4. Webhook `succeeded` fires → activate subscription

### Marketplace Payout

1. Receive payments via SDK or API
2. Call `POST /payout/create` with seller's email and amount
3. Seller receives hash to access payout page
4. Seller withdraws via PayPal, crypto, or gift cards
5. Payout webhook `payout.succeeded` fires → confirm delivery

### Error Handling

```javascript
try {
  await payviox.createSession(params, { redirect: true });
} catch (error) {
  if (error.message.includes('Unauthorized')) {
    // Invalid API key
  } else if (error.message.includes('Bad request')) {
    // Invalid parameters
  } else {
    // Network or server error — retry with backoff
  }
}
```

---

## Redirect-Based Providers (Direct Redirect)

These providers support skipping the Payviox payment page when `paymentMethodId` is set:

| Provider | Example IDs |
|----------|-------------|
| Zen | `zen_card`, `zen_blik` |
| Nicepay | `nicepay_va`, `nicepay_ewallet` |
| PayPal | `paypal` |
| Payssion | Various local methods |
| Pallapay | Crypto |
| Crypto.com | `cryptocom` |

Use `GET /payments/methods` to discover available IDs for your account.
